=== MODELCONTEXTPROTOCOL 1.3.0 - COMPLETE API REFERENCE ===

## 1. MCP SERVICE REGISTRATION (AddMcpServer)

### Method Signature:
IMcpServerBuilder AddMcpServer(
    IServiceCollection services, 
    Action<McpServerOptions> configureOptions)

Location: Microsoft.Extensions.DependencyInjection.McpServerServiceCollectionExtensions.AddMcpServer()
File: /home/runner/.nuget/packages/modelcontextprotocol/1.3.0/lib/net9.0/ModelContextProtocol.dll

Usage Pattern:
var builder = services.AddMcpServer(options => {
    // Configure McpServerOptions here
});

Returns: IMcpServerBuilder - used for chaining WithTools, WithResources, etc.


## 2. TOOL REGISTRATION (WithTools Methods)

### WithTools Generic Methods (Scan Type for [McpServerToolAttribute] methods):

1. WithTools<TToolType>(IMcpServerBuilder, JsonSerializerOptions)
   - Discovers all public/non-public static and instance methods on TToolType
   - Each method must have [McpServerToolAttribute]
   - For instance methods, creates new instance for each tool invocation

2. WithTools<TToolType>(IMcpServerBuilder, TToolType target, JsonSerializerOptions)
   - Same as above, but uses provided 'target' instance for instance methods
   - Reuses single instance across invocations

3. WithTools<TToolType>(IMcpServerBuilder, JsonSerializerOptions) - overload when TToolType is IEnumerable<McpServerTool>
   - If TToolType itself is IEnumerable<McpServerTool>, registers tools directly

### WithTools Non-Generic Methods (Direct Registration):

4. WithTools(IMcpServerBuilder, IEnumerable<McpServerTool> tools)
   - Directly add pre-built McpServerTool instances

5. WithTools(IMcpServerBuilder, IEnumerable<Type> toolTypes, JsonSerializerOptions)
   - Scans multiple types for [McpServerToolAttribute]-marked methods

6. WithToolsFromAssembly(IMcpServerBuilder, Assembly toolAssembly, JsonSerializerOptions)
   - Discovers all types marked with [McpServerToolTypeAttribute] in assembly
   - Then finds methods on those types marked with [McpServerToolAttribute]


## 3. RESOURCE REGISTRATION (WithResources Methods)

### WithResources Generic Methods (Scan Type for [McpServerResourceAttribute] members):

1. WithResources<TResourceType>(IMcpServerBuilder)
   - Discovers all public/non-public static and instance methods/members on TResourceType
   - Each must have [McpServerResourceAttribute]
   - For instance members, creates new instance for each resource invocation

2. WithResources<TResourceType>(IMcpServerBuilder, TResourceType target)
   - Same as above, but uses provided 'target' instance
   - Reuses single instance across invocations

3. Special case: If TResourceType is IEnumerable<McpServerResource>
   - Registers resources directly without method scanning

### WithResources Non-Generic Methods (Direct Registration):

4. WithResources(IMcpServerBuilder, IEnumerable<McpServerResource> resourceTemplates)
   - Directly add pre-built McpServerResource instances

5. WithResources(IMcpServerBuilder, IEnumerable<Type> resourceTemplateTypes)
   - Scans multiple types for [McpServerResourceAttribute]-marked members

6. WithResourcesFromAssembly(IMcpServerBuilder, Assembly resourceAssembly)
   - Discovers all types marked with [McpServerResourceTypeAttribute] in assembly
   - Then finds methods/members on those types marked with [McpServerResourceAttribute]


## 4. ATTRIBUTE SIGNATURES

### [McpServerToolAttribute]
Properties:
  - Name: string (optional) - Tool name
  - Title: string (optional) - Display title
  - Destructive: bool (default: false) - Indicates destructive operation
  - Idempotent: bool (default: false) - Safe to call multiple times
  - OpenWorld: bool (default: false) - Can handle any input
  - ReadOnly: bool (default: false) - Doesn't modify state
  - UseStructuredContent: bool (default: false)
  - OutputSchemaType: Type (optional) - Type for tool output schema
  - IconSource: string (optional) - Icon URI
  - TaskSupport: ToolTaskSupport (optional) - Task execution support
  - TypeId: object (inherited from Attribute)

   - Each must have [McpServerResourceAttribute]
   - For instance members, creates new instance for each resource invocation

2. WithResources<TResourceType>(IMcpServerBuilder, TResourceType target)
   - Same as above, but uses provided 'target' instance
   - Reuses single instance across invocations

3. Special case: If TResourceType is IEnumerable<McpServerResource>
   - Registers resources directly without method scanning

### WithResources Non-Generic Methods (Direct Registration):

4. WithResources(IMcpServerBuilder, IEnumerable<McpServerResource> resourceTemplates)
   - Directly add pre-built McpServerResource instances

5. WithResources(IMcpServerBuilder, IEnumerable<Type> resourceTemplateTypes)
   - Scans multiple types for [McpServerResourceAttribute]-marked members

6. WithResourcesFromAssembly(IMcpServerBuilder, Assembly resourceAssembly)
   - Discovers all types marked with [McpServerResourceTypeAttribute] in assembly
   - Then finds methods/members on those types marked with [McpServerResourceAttribute]


## 4. ATTRIBUTE SIGNATURES

### [McpServerToolAttribute]
Properties:
  - Name: string (optional) - Tool name
  - Title: string (optional) - Display title
  - Destructive: bool (default: false) - Indicates destructive operation
  - Idempotent: bool (default: false) - Safe to call multiple times
  - OpenWorld: bool (default: false) - Can handle any input
  - ReadOnly: bool (default: false) - Doesn't modify state
  - UseStructuredContent: bool (default: false)
  - OutputSchemaType: Type (optional) - Type for tool output schema
  - IconSource: string (optional) - Icon URI
  - TaskSupport: ToolTaskSupport (optional) - Task execution support
  - TypeId: object (inherited from Attribute)

Usage:
[McpServerToolAttribute(Name = "tool_name", Title = "Tool Title")]
public string MyTool(string param1, int param2) { ... }

### [McpServerToolTypeAttribute]
Marks a class to indicate all methods should be scanned for [McpServerToolAttribute]
Properties:
  - TypeId: object (inherited from Attribute)

Usage:
[McpServerToolTypeAttribute]
public class ToolsClass 
{
    [McpServerToolAttribute(Name = "tool1")]
    public string Tool1() { ... }
    
    [McpServerToolAttribute(Name = "tool2")]
    public string Tool2() { ... }
}

### [McpServerResourceAttribute]
Properties:
  - UriTemplate: string (optional) - Resource URI template
  - Name: string (optional) - Resource name
  - Title: string (optional) - Display title
  - MimeType: string (optional) - MIME type of resource
  - IconSource: string (optional) - Icon URI
  - TypeId: object (inherited from Attribute)

Usage:
[McpServerResourceAttribute(UriTemplate = "file://{path}", MimeType = "text/plain")]
public string GetFile(string path) { ... }

### [McpServerResourceTypeAttribute]
Marks a class to indicate all members should be scanned for [McpServerResourceAttribute]
Properties:
  - TypeId: object (inherited from Attribute)

Usage:
[McpServerResourceTypeAttribute]
public class ResourcesClass 
{
    [McpServerResourceAttribute(UriTemplate = "resource1://")]
    public string Resource1() { ... }
    
    [McpServerResourceAttribute(UriTemplate = "resource2://")]
    public string Resource2() { ... }
}


## 5. ASPNETCORE HTTP TRANSPORT SETUP (MapMcp)

### AddMcpServer + WithHttpTransport + MapMcp Pattern

Step 1: Register MCP with HTTP transport in Program.cs
```csharp
builder.Services
    .AddMcpServer(options => { /* configure */ })
    .WithHttpTransport(httpOptions => 
    {
        // Configure HttpServerTransportOptions
        httpOptions.OnSessionStarted = async (session) => { ... };
        httpOptions.OnSessionEnded = async (session) => { ... };
    })
    .WithTools<YourToolsClass>()
    .WithResources<YourResourcesClass>();
```

Step 2: Map MCP endpoints
```csharp
app.MapMcp("/mcp");  // Mounts at /mcp endpoint
```

### WithHttpTransport Method Signature:
IMcpServerBuilder WithHttpTransport(
    IMcpServerBuilder builder,
    Action<HttpServerTransportOptions> configureOptions)

Location: Microsoft.Extensions.DependencyInjection.HttpMcpServerBuilderExtensions.WithHttpTransport()
File: /home/runner/.nuget/packages/modelcontextprotocol.aspnetcore/1.3.0/lib/net9.0/ModelContextProtocol.AspNetCore.dll

### MapMcp Method Signature:
IEndpointConventionBuilder MapMcp(
    IEndpointRouteBuilder endpoints,
    string pattern)

Location: Microsoft.AspNetCore.Builder.McpEndpointRouteBuilderExtensions.MapMcp()
File: /home/runner/.nuget/packages/modelcontextprotocol.aspnetcore/1.3.0/lib/net9.0/ModelContextProtocol.AspNetCore.dll

Parameters:
  - endpoints: IEndpointRouteBuilder (typically 'app' from WebApplication)
  - pattern: string - Route pattern prefix (e.g., "/mcp", "/api/mcp")

Returns: IEndpointConventionBuilder - for configuring endpoint conventions (auth, etc.)

Supports:
  - MCP Streamable HTTP transport (protocol 2025-11-25)
  - Optional legacy SSE endpoints at "/sse" and "/message" when enabled
  - Configurable session lifecycle via HttpServerTransportOptions


## 6. COMPLETE EXAMPLE - HTTP MCP SERVER

```csharp
// Program.cs
using ModelContextProtocol.Server;
using Microsoft.Extensions.DependencyInjection;
using Microsoft.AspNetCore.Builder;

var builder = WebApplication.CreateBuilder(args);

// Define tools class
[McpServerToolTypeAttribute]
public class MyCpuTools 
{
    [McpServerToolAttribute(Name = "cpu_info", Title = "Get CPU Info")]
    public string GetCpuInfo()
    {
        return "CPU Info";
    }
}

// Define resources class
[McpServerResourceTypeAttribute]
public class MyResources 
{
    [McpServerResourceAttribute(
        UriTemplate = "file://{filePath}", 
        MimeType = "text/plain",
        Title = "File Resource")]
    public string GetFile(string filePath)
    {
        return $"Contents of {filePath}";
    }
}

// Register MCP server
builder.Services
    .AddMcpServer(options => 
    {
        options.Name = "My HTTP MCP Server";
        options.Version = "1.0.0";
    })
    .WithHttpTransport(httpOptions =>
    {
        httpOptions.OnSessionStarted = async (session) =>
        {
            Console.WriteLine($"Session started: {session.Id}");
            await Task.CompletedTask;
        };
        httpOptions.OnSessionEnded = async (session) =>
        {
            Console.WriteLine($"Session ended: {session.Id}");
            await Task.CompletedTask;
        };
    })
    .WithTools<MyCpuTools>()
    .WithResources<MyResources>();

var app = builder.Build();

// Map MCP HTTP endpoints at /mcp path
app.MapMcp("/mcp");

app.Run();
```


## 7. REQUIRED PARAMETERS SUMMARY

For Setting Up HTTP MCP Server:

1. **AddMcpServer** - REQUIRED
   - services: IServiceCollection (always provided by builder.Services)
   - configureOptions: Optional callback, but recommended to set Name/Version

2. **WithHttpTransport** - REQUIRED for HTTP servers
   - builder: IMcpServerBuilder (chained from AddMcpServer)
   - configureOptions: Action<HttpServerTransportOptions> - can be empty but common to handle lifecycle

3. **WithTools** - AT LEAST ONE RECOMMENDED
   - builder: IMcpServerBuilder (required)
   - Generic type or instances (required)
   - JsonSerializerOptions: optional for generic methods

4. **WithResources** - OPTIONAL but recommended
   - builder: IMcpServerBuilder (required)
   - Generic type or instances (required)

5. **MapMcp** - REQUIRED to expose endpoints
   - endpoints: IEndpointRouteBuilder (app)
   - pattern: string - route path like "/mcp"
